summary: Russian forces have shot down a new Ukrainian fixed-wing drone, the Klin, which carries up to 5 kg of warhead, has a 120 km range, and features autonomous target capture and air detonation capability. The Russian "Elka" anti-drone system has successfully intercepted multiple Ukrainian fixed-wing drones, including the "Maya" and "Darts," through kinetic impact, triggering onboard charges. Russian operators of the "Rubikon" system reported eliminating 83 Ukrainian targets on March 21 and 94 on March 23, including fixed-wing UAVs and control points. Ukrainian FPV drones continue to strike Russian positions, including a Ka-52 helicopter crew near Nadiivka, and Russian infantry have been recorded attempting to counter drones with bayonets.
